record.field:
Kevin Smith Project

Manufacturing Bom Instruction


1. Frame Fabrication (Laser & Forming)

The frame must be perfectly concentric to prevent the belt from mistracking.

  • Laser Cutting: Load the DXF files for the inner and outer side profiles.

    • Action: Cut the side rails from 10-gauge (3mm) sheet metal. Ensure the arc length ($L$) is calculated as $L = \theta \times R$, where $\theta$ is the angle in radians.

    • Detail: Cut mounting slots for rollers at specific intervals (usually every 5–10 degrees) to allow for minor tracking adjustments.

  • Bending/Rolling: If the design uses a continuous plate rather than faceted segments:

    • Action: Run the side rails through a plate roller to match the precise radius of the bed.

  • Cross-Member Prep: Cut C-channel or square tubing to the exact "width" of the conveyor.

    • Action: Deburr all edges to prevent belt snagging.

2. Bed Plate & Slider Bed Assembly

This is the surface the belt slides on. It must be smooth and low-friction.

  • Sheet Cutting: Laser cut the "fan-shaped" bed sections.

    • Action: If the conveyor is wide, cut the bed into 30-degree segments to reduce material waste (nesting).

  • Countersinking: * Action: Drill and countersink holes into the bed plate so that the flat-head screws sit below the surface of the metal.

    • Safety Check: Any protruding bolt head will tear the belt within minutes of operation.

3. Tapered Roller Machining

Standard straight rollers will not work; the outer edge of the belt travels faster than the inner edge.

  • Lathe Turning: Take high-density polyethylene (HDPE) or steel tube blanks.

    • Action: Turn the tubes on a lathe to create a taper. The ratio must match the radii:

      $$\frac{\text{Diameter}_{outer}}{\text{Diameter}_{inner}} = \frac{R_{outer}}{R_{inner}}$$

  • Bearing Press: * Action: Press-fit precision ball bearings into both ends of the tapered rollers. Insert a through-shaft (typically 12mm or 15mm hex or round).

4. Drive & Tail Pulley Integration

  • Lagging the Drive Pulley:

    • Action: Apply a rubber "lagging" (vulcanized or glue-on) to the drive pulley. This prevents the belt from slipping under load.

  • Shaft Alignment: * Action: Mount the drive and tail shafts into the side frames using flanged bearings.

    • Precision Step: Use a laser level to ensure the drive shaft is perfectly perpendicular to the center-line of the curve.

5. Belt Preparation & Guidance

  • Edge Beading: * Action: High-frequency weld a PVC "V-guide" or "Bead" to the underside of the outer radius of the belt.

  • Track Installation: * Action: Install a UHMW (Ultra-High Molecular Weight) plastic guide track along the outer side frame. The belt bead will "lock" into this track to counteract the centripetal force pulling the belt inward.

6. Final Assembly & Squaring

  • Frame Squaring: * Action: Bolt the cross-members to the side rails. Measure the distance between the inner and outer rails at the start, middle, and end of the curve. The variance must be $< 1mm$.

  • Belt Tensioning:

    • Action: Thread the belt through the rollers and around the drive/tail pulleys.

    • Tracking Run: Power on the motor at a low frequency (10–15Hz). Observe the belt. Adjust the take-up bolts on the tail pulley until the belt stays centered in the guide track.

7. Electrical & Safety

  • Motor Mounting: Bolt the gearmotor to the drive shaft torque arm.

  • Wiring: Connect the motor to the VFD.

  • Guard Installation: * Action: Install "Finger Guards" at the pinch points where the belt meets the end rollers. These should be cut from 1/8" clear polycarbonate for visibility.
